change: you change the commit message format

impact.why: The wire type itself: CommonPayload + Digest + CommitSeal. Changing commit format = changing these fields.
Constructor used by broadcastCommit; field set / arg list changes with the format.
Encode counterpart: serializes [Sequence,Round,Digest,CommitSeal]+signature; the wire layout.
Decode counterpart on the receiver: anonymous struct field order must mirror EncodeRLP exactly.
Defines the signed-over byte layout; if commit fields change, the signing payload (and thus signature verification) changes.
Receiver/consumer: reads commit.Digest, commit.Round, commit.CommitSeal, commit.Source(); verifies seal and accumulates to quorum. Mirrors broadcastCommit.
Aggregates each commit's CommitSeal into committedSeals and calls backend.Commit; consumes the commit-message seal field.
Routes CommitCode -> handleCommitMsg with type assertion to *wbfmessage.Commit; the dispatch contract for the commit message.
Seal verification invoked by handleCommitMsg on the commit's CommitSeal; if the seal format/round encoding changes, verification must change.
Builds the seal bytes signed in broadcastCommit (SealTypeCommit) and re-derived in verifySeal; producer/verifier of the commit seal contents.
Sole caller: handlePrepareMsg triggers broadcastCommit on prepare quorum; sequencing context for the commit phase.

grep_would_miss: verifySeal/PrepareSeal in core.go — grep for 'broadcastCommit' or 'Commit' message terms misses the BLS seal contract that the commit message carries.
deliverMessage CommitCode dispatch — coupled by the *wbfmessage.Commit type assertion, not by the seed name.
EncodePayloadForSigning — the signed-payload layout is a separate contract from the RLP wire layout; both must change together.
notes: Seed broadcastCommit (commit.go:36) excluded as definition. The commit 'format' is two coupled contracts: the RLP wire layout (EncodeRLP/DecodeRLP) and the signed payload (EncodePayloadForSigning + PrepareSeal/SealTypeCommit). handleCommitMsg + commitWBFT are the receive-side counterparts. backend.Commit (consumes committedSeals) is downstream but its signature is seal-list based, not commit-message-format based, so not included. cks edges empty; built from ripgrep + Read.
